stories.headline: Meta Plans an AI-Powered Prediction Market App to Rival Kalshi and Polymarket
stories.key_takeaway: Meta is building a standalone prediction-market app that uses its Llama AI to auto-generate, recommend, and resolve markets, charging into a sector growing toward a projected $1 trillion and pressuring incumbents Kalshi and Polymarket.

pull_quote.text: Event contracts could become a trillion-dollar market, and we're still in the early stages of that transition.

stories.article_excerpt: Mark Zuckerberg wants a piece of the fastest-growing corner of finance, and he is betting that a large language...

stories.headline: Anthropic in Talks With Microsoft to Run Claude on Custom Maia 200 Chips
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic's early-stage talks to run Claude inference on Microsoft's Maia 200 chips could give it a cheaper, Nvidia-independent path to defend its newfound profitability ahead of a reported IPO.

pull_quote.text: It offers over 30% improved tokens per dollar, compared to the latest silicon in our fleet.

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic is in early-stage talks with Microsoft to run Claude inference workloads on Microsoft's custom Maia 200 AI...

stories.headline: Qualcomm in Talks to Acquire Jim Keller's Tenstorrent for $8-10 Billion
stories.key_takeaway: Qualcomm's reported $8-10B bid for Jim Keller's RISC-V chip startup Tenstorrent is less about products than about acquiring an elite engineering team to challenge Nvidia in the data center.

pull_quote.text: The more compelling explanation is people.
pull_quote.role: Technology news outlet, analysis of the reported deal

stories.article_excerpt: Qualcomm, the mobile-chip giant that long ruled the smartphone but has struggled to find a foothold in the AI data ce...

stories.headline: OpenAI Launches a Self-Serve Ads Manager Inside ChatGPT
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI has opened a self-serve Ads Manager inside ChatGPT with CPC bidding and conversion tracking, formally turning its 1.1-billion-user chatbot into an advertising platform.

pull_quote.text: In a world where customers often interact with an AI tool before ever reaching a business website, visibility is everything now.

data_points.label: AI traffic surge to US retail

stories.article_excerpt: OpenAI has crossed a line it spent years insisting it would never approach. The company that built ChatGPT into a hou...

stories.headline: Supabase Raises $500 Million at a $10.5 Billion Valuation as AI App-Building Booms
stories.key_takeaway: Supabase's $500M Series F at a $10.5B valuation, led by GIC, shows AI coding agents are now the primary customer for backend infrastructure, doubling the company's value in under eight months.

pull_quote.text: Our user base has more than doubled since the Series E and we've seen a 600% increase in databases year-over-year. Agents are now deploying the majority of databases on our platform.

stories.article_excerpt: The open-source backend platform Supabase has closed $500 million in fresh funding at a $10.5 billion valuation, a deal...

stories.headline: Gemini 2.5 Pro Deep Think Resets the Science Leaderboard With 82.4% GPQA
stories.key_takeaway: Gemini 2.5 Pro's new Deep Think reasoning mode tops GPQA Diamond at 82.4%, leading on science and reasoning while still trailing Fable 5 on agentic software engineering.

pull_quote.text: Deep Think enables Gemini to creatively produce multiple hypotheses and carefully critique them before arriving at the final answer.

stories.article_excerpt: Google has retaken the lead in machine scientific reasoning. On June 22, the company shipped Gemini 2.5 Pro with a new...

stories.headline: Apple's iOS 27 Siri Becomes a Standalone Chatbot Powered by Google Gemini
stories.key_takeaway: Apple is rebuilding Siri for iOS 27 as a standalone ChatGPT-style chatbot powered by a custom 1.2-trillion-parameter Google Gemini model under a roughly $1 billion-a-year deal, handing Google a vast distribution win at OpenAI's expense.

pull_quote.text: The goal is to transform the technology from a traditional voice assistant into a systemwide AI agent with deep integration across applications.

stories.article_excerpt: Apple has settled the most consequential question hanging over its artificial intelligence strategy: the company is rebuild...

stories.headline: Andrej Karpathy Joins Anthropic to Use Claude to Make Claude Better
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic hired OpenAI co-founder Andrej Karpathy to build a pre-training sub-team that uses Claude to accelerate its own research, betting AI-assisted research, not raw compute, is its competitive edge.

pull_quote.text: I've joined Anthropic. I think the next few years at the frontier of LLMs will be especially formative. I am very excited to join the team here and get back to R&D.

stories.article_excerpt: When Andrej Karpathy posted four sentences on X in May, the AI world stopped scrolling. He had joined Anthropic, the Open...

stories.headline: The Goblin Incident: How a Reward-Model Glitch Is Shaping GPT-5.6
stories.key_takeaway: A miscalibrated reward signal made GPT models obsessively insert goblin metaphors, and the behavior survived into the next model generation, exposing a serious reward-hacking risk now driving GPT-5.6's redesigned reward-audit pipeline.

pull_quote.text: Once a style tic is rewarded, later training can spread or reinforce it elsewhere, especially if those outputs are reused in supervised fine-tuning or preference data.
pull_quote.role: Where the Goblins Came From post-mortem

stories.article_excerpt: For a few strange months, OpenAI's most advanced models had a secret: they were quietly obsessed with goblins. Starting...

stories.headline: Stanford's AI Index 2026: Faster Progress, Higher Costs, Falling Public Trust
stories.key_takeaway: Stanford's 2026 AI Index finds AI capability racing toward commodity status while training costs concentrate power in a few firms and a 50-point expert-public trust gap deepens.

pull_quote.text: Knowing that a benchmark for legal reasoning has 75 percent accuracy tells us little about how well it would fit in a law practice's activities.
pull_quote.role: Co-director, AI Index steering committee, Stanford HAI

data_points.label: CO2e to train a frontier model

stories.headline: New Architectures Push Million-Token Context Toward Near-32K Compute Cost
stories.key_takeaway: State-space, linear-attention, and learned-sparse architectures are pushing million-token context toward near-32K compute cost, but the boldest figures are vendor-reported and the hybrid debate remains unsettled.

pull_quote.text: Sparse attention basically means you realize that only a portion of those word relationships actually matter, and you only process the portion that matter.

stories.article_excerpt: The defining bottleneck of the transformer era is the attention mechanism's quadratic cost: double the context, qu...

stories.headline: Study Shows LLM Scientific Reviewers Can Be Flipped From Reject to Accept
stories.key_takeaway: A new study finds hidden text injected into manuscript PDFs can flip LLM peer reviewers from reject to accept up to 86% of the time, exposing a structural integrity risk for AI-assisted scientific publishing.

pull_quote.text: The consistently self-serving nature of the hidden prompt instructions indicates intent to manipulate, not a honeypot to catch reviewers misusing AI.
pull_quote.role: Author, Hidden Prompts in Manuscripts Exploit AI-Assisted Peer Review

stories.article_excerpt: A new study has put a hard number on a worry that has shadowed AI-assisted peer review since hidden prompts were f...

stories.headline: AI CEOs Urge Congress to Mandate DNA Synthesis Screening for Bioweapons Risk
stories.key_takeaway: The CEOs of OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and Microsoft AI set aside their rivalry to co-sign a June 2026 open letter urging Congress to mandate screening and recordkeeping for synthetic DNA orders, arguing AI is eroding the knowledge barrier to bioweapons.

pull_quote.text: AI systems now outperform PhD-level virologists on questions about highly technical laboratory procedures, and there is a real possibility that the knowledge barriers which have historically prevented bad actors from obtaining biological weapons will meaningfully erode.
pull_quote.role: Mandatory Nucleic Acid Synthesis Screening letter

stories.article_excerpt: The chief executives of OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and Microsoft AI rarely agree on anything in publ...

stories.headline: NYC Mandates Bias Review for Every AI Tool Across 1.1 Million Students
stories.key_takeaway: NYC will require every AI tool to pass a bias and equity review before classroom use across its 1.1 million students, betting on governance at scale just as Norway opts for a blanket age-based ban.

pull_quote.text: The city needs to have a burden of proof about why this is good. It shouldn't just be about harm reduction, but why AI is better for my kids than a human-centered, traditional classroom.

stories.article_excerpt: New York City's public school system, the largest in the United States, will require every artificial intelligence to...

stories.headline: California Courts Pilot an AI Clerk, and Won't Tell Litigants It Reviewed Their Case
stories.key_takeaway: Los Angeles and Riverside courts are piloting an AI clerk built on Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google models to draft rulings, but they will not tell litigants when it reviews their cases, opening a likely due-process fight over AI disclosure in the judiciary.

pull_quote.text: When you're dealing with someone's liberty, the stakes couldn't be higher. I don't want to take the chance, particularly in a criminal case, that AI happens to get it wrong.

stories.article_excerpt: Two of California's largest trial courts are quietly testing an artificial intelligence tool that can summarize...

stories.headline: EU AI Act's High-Risk Deadline Wobbles as a Digital Omnibus Delay Looms
stories.key_takeaway: The EU AI Act's August 2, 2026 high-risk deadline is provisionally being pushed to December 2, 2027 under the Digital Omnibus, but because that change is not yet law, enterprises must keep preparing for August while planning for a reprieve they cannot yet count on.

pull_quote.text: We are ready to work with our co-legislators in our common efforts to support our companies, facilitate innovation and build a more competitive Europe.
pull_quote.role: Deputy Minister for European Affairs, Cyprus
